PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE CALENDAR FOR ATTORNEY JAMES B. EWBANK
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Inactivate Attorney

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY JAMES B. EWBANK
Off Calendar Oct-10-2019 Continued To Nov-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sean E. Makarin, Esq., Parsley & Makharin, 415-777-1800, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONERS GUILLAUME GASSUAN, TODD MORENO'S Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees

Petitioners Guillaume Gassuan and Todd M. Moreno's motion for an award of reasonable attorney fees is granted in the total amount of $16,613.70.

The award is made pursuant to Civil Code section 1717 and Section 9.400 of the Laws of the Benevolent and Protective Order of Elks of the United States. By amendment dated July 3, 2018, Respondent BPOE amended Section 9.400 to provide in pertinent part as follows: "In any action brought by the Benevolent and Protective Order of Elks of the United States of America against any Member, Lodge or State Association, such Member, Lodge or State Association . . . further agrees that in any such action, upon entry of judgment in favor of the Benevolent and Protective Order of Elks of the United States, the offending Member, Lodge or State Association shall be responsible for reimbursement of all costs and reasonable attorney's fees incurred." The parties agree that this provision constitutes an enforceable contract between the BPOE and its members. (See, e.g., Thatcher v. City Terrace Cultural Center (1960) 181 Cal.App.2d 433, 443.) Respondent BPOE does not dispute that although the amendment on its face is a unilateral provision that permits only the BPOE to recover its fees if it prevails in an action brought against a member, Section 1717 renders the provision mutual. (e.g., Scott Co. of California v. Blount, Inc. (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1103, 1106 [section 1717 "provides that any contractual attorney fees provision must be applied mutually and equally to all parties to the contract, even if it is written otherwise"].) Nor does Respondent dispute that Petitioners prevailed in this action by virtue of the Court's January 30, 2019 order.

Respondents contend that because the amendment of Section 9.400 did not become effective until August 3, 2018, it was not part of the contract between Petitioners and the BPOE at the time Petitioners' claims arose, and Petitioners therefore are not entitled to seek attorney fees under that provision. Not so. A new statute authorizing an award of attorney fees applies to actions not yet final at the time the statute becomes effective. (California Housing Finance Agency v. E.R. Fairway Associates I (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 1508, 1513.) The same principle logically applies here, since the new bylaw became effective before the Court issued its order in Petitioners' favor, and there is no language in Section 9.400 that suggests any intent to limit its application to actions filed after its effective date. Respondents also challenge the amount of fees and costs sought by Petitioners as unreasonable. The Court disagrees, and finds both the amount of time for which compensation is sought and the hourly fees requested eminently reasonable. Respondents' objection to certain costs sought by Petitioners, including costs of photocopies and transcripts, lacks merit. Section 9.400 entitles the prevailing party to recover "all costs" incurred, not just those authorized by Code of Civil Procedure section 1033.5. (See Arntz Contracting v. St. Paul Fire & Marine (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 464, 492.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Order To Show Cause Re: Alternative Writ Of Mandate (Per Order Filed 9/20/18)
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sean Makarin, Esq. and Walter Parsley, Esq. 415-777-1800 for Petitioner.
Michael Osborne, Esq. 650-291-1446 and James Ewbank, Esq. II 512-476-1080 of Cokinos for Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court clarifies that both sides do not need to present live evidence/testimony, and asks pointed questions. Plaintiff argues procedures fundamentally unfair and void. The Court asks questions prior to recess - Besides, the issue of Under CA law was Petitioner entitled to a process prior to the executive order, are there any other issues?

The matter is recalled at 11:20. Both sides address the Court's questions. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:
